evidence: Package hierarchy follows a clean top-down direction: api → exploration/query → config → model → utils. No reverse imports observed.
LongIntHashMap (utils) is a self-contained leaf; it imports only java.util.Arrays — zero coupling outward.
The api package imports from exploration, query, config, and model — all downstream. No upstream pull.
exploration imports from query, config, and model but never from api — direction is maintained.
CollocateQueryHelper is package-private inside query/, functioning as an internal helper without leaking out.
WordSketchApiServer is the wiring hub for all handler classes; changes to HttpApiUtils or a shared handler pattern have blast radius across the entire api/ package (10 classes).
RadialPlot (rendering) and ExploreResponseBuilder (presentation translation) coexist with HTTP handlers in api/ — three distinct concerns in one flat package, all importing from each other's layer.
No import cycles detected across packages.

strengths: Strict top-down import discipline: no reverse dependencies between packages (api never imported by exploration/query/config).
CollocateQueryHelper is correctly scoped package-private so the boundary does not leak.
LongIntHashMap is a cleanly isolated leaf component with no outward coupling.
exploration/ and query/ are separate packages with no coupling between them, cleanly separating semantic algorithm from corpus access.
The single-root Main.java wires everything at startup and is the only caller of both api and query layers together — an intentionally narrow coupling point.
cross_module_architecture.issue_character: The main structural concern is the api/ package acting as a flat hub that mixes HTTP transport, presentation translation, and rendering — this is a concentration issue rather than a direction violation.
cross_module_architecture.score_rationale: The codebase has no dependency cycles and enforces a clean top-down direction throughout all packages. The only architectural concern is the api/ package growing to 10 files with three loosely related concerns (HTTP handlers, ExploreResponseBuilder, RadialPlot) sharing the same namespace, widening blast radius for common changes. This is a moderate concentration problem rather than a boundary violation, placing the score in the solid-but-uneven range.

issues.summary: api/ package mixes HTTP handlers, response translation, and rendering in one flat namespace
evidence: api/ holds 10 files: 5 HTTP handlers (Sketch, Concordance, Bcql, Exploration, Visualization), 1 server (WordSketchApiServer), 1 HTTP utility (HttpApiUtils), 1 response builder (ExploreResponseBuilder), 1 rendering class (RadialPlot), 1 route-wiring seam.
RadialPlot generates SVG output and has no HTTP-specific logic; it is a rendering component.
ExploreResponseBuilder translates model objects to JSON maps — presentation logic, not transport.
issues.suggestion: Extract a presentation/ or response/ sub-package for ExploreResponseBuilder and RadialPlot. HTTP handler classes remain in api/. This separates transport from presentation and reduces blast radius for changes to either concern.

root_causes: api/ package has absorbed all new classes added during feature work without sub-package extraction
likely_symptoms: api-hub-overload shows as a package flat_dir_issues signal
